stories.headline: Africa's GDP growth to stabilise at 4.3% in 2026, 4.5% in 2027: AfDB
stories.summary: Africa's real gross domestic product (GDP) growth is projected to stabilise at 4.3% in 2026 and grow further to 4.5% in 2027, according to the African Development Bank (AfDB) Group. The AfDB's 2026 Africa Macroeconomic Performance and Outlook report provides this forecast. In 2025, 12 of the world's 20 fastest-growing economies were in Africa, indicating the continent's strong economic performance. The projected growth rates for 2026 and 2027 suggest a continued positive trend for Africa's economy. The AfDB's report highlights the continent's potential for sustained economic growth, with the projected growth rates expected to stabilise and increase over the next two years. This forecast is based on the AfDB's analysis of Africa's macroeconomic performance and outlook.

stories.headline: High price of petrol takes its toll in South-East Asia
stories.summary: Fuel prices have surged across Southeast Asia due to global oil costs and geopolitical tensions. In Thailand, motorists faced the steepest fuel-price increases in decades after the government reduced subsidies. Laos' diesel price increase is attributed to its weakened currency against the US dollar. Singapore saw a 29% increase in diesel prices. The rising fuel costs are affecting various sectors, including farming. In Thailand's rice paddies, farmers are struggling to keep water pumps running due to fuel shortages and high prices. The situation is prompting countries to take action to ensure fuel supply and keep prices in check, with measures such as closing schools and imposing daily limits. The crisis is having a significant impact on daily life, commuting costs, food prices, and household budgets across the region.

stories.headline: NASA releases stunning first photos of Earth from Artemis II moon mission
stories.summary: NASA has released the first images taken from the Artemis II Orion spacecraft, showing Earth from space. The photos, taken by mission commander Reid Wiseman, depict the planet swathed in swirling clouds, rising through a window on the Orion capsule. The images were captured using Wiseman's Personal Computing Device, a tablet with a camera, and are the first to be downlinked from the Artemis II astronauts. The crew of four is currently traveling on a mission around the moon, having passed the halfway point between Earth and the moon. The high-resolution images provide a stunning view of Earth from space, with NASA describing them as "spectacular." The release of these photos marks a significant milestone in the Artemis II mission, which aims to journey around the moon with a crew on board.

stories.headline: The ongoing war in the Middle East poses an existential crisis for some of the poorest people in the world, many of whom are already hanging on by a thread.
stories.summary: The ongoing war in the Middle East poses a significant threat to the poorest people in the region, exacerbating their already dire situation. The Iranian president has warned that continuing hostility towards Iran will be costly and futile. Meanwhile, in Israel, Passover celebrations have continued, with some marked underground. The conflict has far-reaching implications, affecting countries including Egypt, Syria, Lebanon, Yemen, and the Gulf states. The region is of immense strategic, cultural, and human significance. The US president has taken military action, and the situation remains volatile. Oil prices are also a concern, with the fuel shock affecting millions in South-East Asia and Australians. The Middle East conflict encompasses the Iran war, Gaza ceasefire, Israel-Palestine conflict, and diplomacy, with the international community closely monitoring developments. The region's instability has severe consequences for its most vulnerable populations.

stories.headline: Anthropic says Claude subscriptions will no longer support OpenClaw because it puts an 'outsized strain' on systems
stories.summary: Anthropic will no longer support the use of Claude subscriptions with third-party tools, including OpenClaw, starting April 4, 2026. The company stated that using Claude subscriptions with these tools violates its terms of service and puts an "outsized strain" on its systems. Anthropic claims that this strain affects its infrastructure and that capacity must be prioritized for customers using its core products. The decision was announced via a notification email, citing the need to allocate resources efficiently. OpenClaw's creator, Peter Steinberger, was informed of the change, which will impact users who currently use Claude subscriptions with the tool. Anthropic's move aims to optimize its systems and ensure a better experience for its core product users. The change will take effect at 12 pm PT/3 pm ET on April 4, 2026.

stories.headline: Apple's Next iPad Mini 8 Rumored for Late 2026 OLED Upgrade and Performance Boost
stories.summary: Apple's next iPad Mini 8 is rumored to launch in late 2026 with significant upgrades. The device is expected to feature an OLED display, a major overhaul from its predecessor. On the performance front, the iPad Mini 8 may be powered by an A18 Pro or even more advanced chip, paired with at least 8GB of RAM. This would enhance support for demanding Apple Intelligence features in iPadOS 26 or future versions. The new iPad Mini is also expected to have improved water resistance and a sleek design. Pricing is expected to be higher, starting at $599. The OLED display and performance boost are expected to improve on-device AI processing for writing tools, image generation, and multitasking. The launch is anticipated to take place later this year, with some reports suggesting a significant display overhaul and upgraded performance.

stories.headline: Microsoft CTO Kevin Scott: Single person at Microsoft who holds people most accountable is …
stories.summary: Microsoft CTO Kevin Scott revealed that CFO Amy Hood is the single person at the company who holds people most accountable. In an interview with Bloomberg, Scott praised Hood's role in maintaining accountability within the organization. As the company's chief financial officer, Hood plays a crucial role in overseeing Microsoft's financial operations and strategy. Scott's statement highlights her influence and importance in ensuring that employees are held responsible for their actions and performance. This acknowledgment comes as Microsoft continues to navigate the tech industry and invest in emerging technologies like artificial intelligence. Scott's comments demonstrate the value the company places on accountability and effective leadership.

stories.headline: Scopely Releases Standalone MONOPOLY GO!Chat App Scopely launched MONOPOLY GO!Chat worldwide with direct messaging, group chats up to 20 players, and more
stories.summary: Scopely has launched a standalone MONOPOLY GO!Chat app worldwide. The app allows users to engage in direct messaging and group chats with up to 20 players. This release is part of Scopely's expansion efforts, driven by the success of Monopoly GO!, which accounts for 73.97% of the company's revenue. In 2025, Scopely generated $115.1 million in revenue, with aggressive monetization strategies and IP-based games contributing to its growth. The new chat app is expected to further enhance the gaming experience for MONOPOLY GO! users, providing a dedicated platform for social interaction and community building. The launch of the MONOPOLY GO!Chat app demonstrates Scopely's commitment to delivering engaging and interactive experiences for its users.

stories.headline: Coaches push back after LeBron James suggests NBA better if Grizzlies left Memphis
stories.summary: Los Angeles Lakers star LeBron James suggested the Memphis Grizzlies should relocate to Nashville, prompting a defensive response from NBA head coaches. Memphis Grizzlies coach Taylor Jenkins, not Tuomas Iisalo, and Toronto Raptors coach Darko Rajakovic, who was an assistant in Memphis, pushed back against James' comments. They defended Memphis and its culture, implying that the city is a viable and supportive home for the Grizzlies. The coaches' response came on Friday, following James' recent criticism. The Grizzlies have been based in Memphis since 2001, and the city has a strong fan base and rich basketball history. The coaches' statements highlighted the importance of the team to the community and the city's ability to support a professional sports franchise.

stories.headline: Man City could face Premier League fixture headache as FA Cup complication emerges
stories.summary: Manchester City's FA Cup progress may cause a Premier League fixture backlog. The team has a hectic schedule with nine games in five weeks, potentially disrupting their league campaign. A postponed home match against Crystal Palace, initially scheduled for the penultimate weekend in March, still needs to be rearranged. City's participation in the Carabao Cup final led to the postponement, and the fixture clash is complicated by Palace's involvement in the Conference League. Palace faces Fiorentina in the quarter-final on April 9 and 16, adding to the scheduling challenges. Manchester City's upcoming FA Cup quarter-final against Liverpool may further impact their fixture list, with the team already having played one game fewer than some of their league rivals, including Arsenal. The congested schedule may pose a challenge for City's pursuit of the domestic treble.

stories.headline: Minister optimistic new FAM team can restore football’s glory
stories.summary: Malaysia's Minister is optimistic that a new Football Association of Malaysia (FAM) team can restore the country's football glory. The current FAM leadership has been marred by controversy, including a penalty from Fifa last September for submitting falsified documents to confirm the eligibility of seven naturalised Malaysian footballers. The penalty was imposed before Malaysia's 2027 Asian Cup qualifier against Vietnam in June. FAM is planning to hold elections in August or September to appoint a new leadership for the 2025-2029 term. The Minister believes that a new leadership can address existing weaknesses and improve the national team's dignity. The appointment of a new team is expected to pave the way for efforts to uplift the Harimau Malaya, Malaysia's national football team. The elections are seen as a crucial step towards restoring the country's football reputation.

stories.headline: Chelsea must avoid dreaded FA Cup upset vs Port Vale: Where to watch the match online, live stream, TV channels, and kick-off time
stories.summary: Chelsea faces Port Vale in an FA Cup quarter-final match at Stamford Bridge, seeking to avoid a fifth straight loss under head coach Liam Rosenior. The match is available to stream online for subscribers via the Discovery+ app or website. Chelsea aims to avoid an upset against the third-tier outfit Port Vale. The Blues are looking to bounce back from recent losses, while Port Vale seeks to pull off a major upset. The match is part of the FA Cup quarter-finals, with the winner potentially advancing to the semi-finals. Team news and possible lineups have been previewed, with Liam Rosenior looking to win his first match as Chelsea manager. The kick-off time and TV channels for the match have been announced, allowing fans to watch the highly anticipated clash.

stories.headline: 9 members of a family, including 6 children, dead as car falls into water-filled well
stories.summary: Nine members of a family, including six children, died after their car fell into a water-filled well in Dindori, Nashik district, Maharashtra. The incident occurred around 11:30 pm on Friday as the family was returning home from an annual function. The vehicle veered off the Nashik-Vani Road and plunged into the well, resulting in the tragic loss of lives. The family had been out for a gathering and were heading back home when the accident took place, about 40 km from Nashik. The accident raises concerns about road safety and infrastructure in rural areas. Seven people, including three men, three women, and a two-year-old boy, were among those killed in the devastating accident. The incident is a tragic reminder of the importance of road safety measures, particularly in rural areas with poor infrastructure.

stories.headline: West Bengal elections: EC set to deploy 2.4 lakh CAPF personnel, 500 companies to stay back after results
stories.summary: The Election Commission of India will deploy 2.4 lakh Central Armed Police Force (CAPF) personnel for the West Bengal assembly elections. The state will go to polls in two phases on April 23 and 29, with counting of votes on May 4. To ensure post-poll security, 200 companies will guard EVMs and counting centers. Additionally, 500 companies will remain deployed in the state until the completion of counting. The increased deployment is due to the reduced number of phases, from six to two, requiring more personnel per phase. The EC has also taken measures to ensure free and fair elections, including removing senior West Bengal officials and banning politicians from entering Cooch Behar district for 72 hours after recent violence.

stories.headline: 'Anxiety among minorities': Shashi Tharoor questions FCRA Ammendment Bill
stories.summary: Shashi Tharoor has questioned the FCRA Amendment Bill, citing concerns that it may create anxiety among religious minorities. The opposition has vowed to remain vigilant and prevent the passage of any amendment that could negatively impact these groups. Tharoor's remarks came amidst massive opposition protests against the bill. The opposition has stressed that they will not allow the passage of any amendment that creates anxiety among religious minorities. The bill's potential impact on minority communities has sparked widespread concern and debate. The opposition's stance indicates a strong resistance to the bill, with a focus on protecting the rights and interests of religious minorities. The bill's fate remains uncertain, with the opposition poised to closely scrutinize its provisions and potential consequences.

stories.headline: A decade and 61 cases later, Supreme Court grants divorce to end matrimonial dispute
stories.summary: The Supreme Court has granted divorce to a couple, ending a decade-long matrimonial dispute. The court settled all claims between the couple and quashed 61 related cases. This decision comes after the couple held irreconcilably opposing views on their marriage and refused to accommodate each other. The court's ruling is in line with its previous stance that when spouses cannot reconcile, their conduct itself amounts to cruelty, justifying divorce. The case, which spanned 10 years, has finally been resolved, bringing an end to the lengthy and complex legal battle between the couple. The Supreme Court's decision aims to provide closure and a sense of finality to the couple, allowing them to move forward with their lives. The ruling also highlights the court's approach to dealing with matrimonial disputes and its emphasis on the importance of reconciliation.

stories.headline: Geopolitics of the Third World War Examine how the Third World War (geopolitics) reflects a radical shift from traditional world politics to a new multipolar order.
stories.summary: The Third World War reflects a radical shift from traditional world politics to a new multipolar order. International institutions, still organized according to the Westphalian system and bipolar world, no longer correspond to the current state of affairs. The concept of a Third World War is becoming increasingly relevant, with global events such as Russia's conflict with Ukraine, Israel's involvement in the Middle East, and the US-China rivalry over Taiwan. The global economy is also being reshaped by sanctions and counter-sanctions. Recent events in Venezuela are part of this larger geopolitical landscape, prompting a reevaluation of outdated frameworks for understanding global politics. The world is experiencing a significant transformation, with multiple powers vying for influence, marking a departure from the traditional bipolar world order. This new multipolar order is characterized by increased complexity and competition.

stories.headline: ME’s Point of Inflection - Part 1 The US–Israel war on Iran has brought geopolitics in the Middle East (ME), the Greater Middle East Region (GMER), and perhaps the world to a defining inflection
stories.summary: The US and Israel have launched a war on Iran, conducting air attacks across the country in an attempt to bring about regime change. The conflict has plunged the Middle East region into a potentially prolonged crisis. The US plans to continue strikes on Iran, with a reported pause on attacks on Tehran's energy sites. Recent developments include air strikes killing 15 PMF fighters in Iraq's Anbar province and Saudi Arabia intercepting over 20 drones in the eastern part of the country. The situation is ongoing, with the potential for further escalation and a lasting impact on the region. The conflict is being closely monitored, with updates and analysis provided by news outlets such as the BBC and Al Jazeera. The region remains on high alert, with the international community watching for further developments.

stories.headline: Why Losing the Iran War May Be the Best Outcome for the Global Economy Structural energy shortages, not geopolitics alone, are driving global conflict, and a U.S. defeat in Iran could accelerate a necessary economic reorganization
stories.summary: The US-Iran war has significant economic, political, and military costs for the region and global economy. Structural energy shortages, rather than geopolitics alone, are driving global conflict. A US defeat in Iran could accelerate a necessary economic reorganization towards reduced energy consumption. The war has drawn comparisons to the 1970s oil shocks, which led to stagflation. The Persian Gulf's economic model is under threat, while China may benefit from a global shift to renewable energy. The US economy is also affected, with economists considering the uneven distribution of economic consequences worldwide. The war's economic costs are mounting, with lives lost and significant economic repercussions in its second week. The global economy's response to the conflict will be crucial, with potential long-term implications for energy consumption and economic restructuring.

stories.headline: There are only 3 headlines provided, therefore only 3 can be returned.
stories.summary: Google's potential rewriting of headlines may significantly reduce search traffic to content creators' websites, impacting their ad revenue. This move is seen as an intervention in journalism, rather than just organizing the web. Meanwhile, other news includes people struggling with rising gas costs, with some splurging while others see their refunds depleted. In space exploration, NASA's Artemis II mission is set to take astronauts Wiseman, Glover, Koch, and Hansen on a 10-day journey around the Moon. The mission will utilize the Orion spacecraft. Additionally, local news outlets like THV11 are providing updates on Arkansas news, weather, and national headlines, with broadcasts scheduled for 10pm. These stories highlight various developments in technology, economy, and space exploration.

stories.headline: High price of petrol takes its toll in South-East Asia The ongoing war in the Middle East poses an existential crisis for some of the poorest people in the world, many of whom are already hanging on by a thread.
stories.summary: The ongoing war in the Middle East is causing a fuel crisis, severely impacting South-East Asia's poorest people. The conflict has led to higher oil prices, with petrol and diesel prices soaring. In some countries, it now costs over £100 to fill a family car. The US, which bombed Iran in late February, has threatened further action, potentially prolonging the war and raising fuel prices. In response, countries like South Korea and Thailand are setting limits on fuel charges to cushion the blow. The crisis is deepening, with millions in South-East Asia affected by the high fuel prices, exacerbating existing economic struggles. The international community is responding to the crisis, with various measures being implemented to mitigate its impact on finances and daily life. The situation remains volatile, with further developments expected.

stories.headline: Department of Labour data shows Apple Meta, Google and Amazon have slashed H
stories.summary: Department of Labour data reveals that Apple, Meta, Google, and Amazon have significantly reduced their H-1B visa petitions. The decline is almost half compared to the previous year. This decrease occurred in the first quarter of fiscal 2026, which spans from October to December. The reduction in H-1B applications is attributed to stricter immigration rules and recent tech industry layoffs. According to the data, these major tech companies filed markedly fewer H-1B visa applications compared to the same period in the previous year. The decline reflects the current state of the tech industry and the impact of changing immigration policies on major employers like Apple, Meta, Google, and Amazon. The data indicates a clear drop in H-1B petitions, signaling a shift in the hiring practices of these prominent tech companies.

stories.headline: Italian court orders Netflix to refund seven years' worth of price increases Saying the increases violated Italy's consumer code, the court ordered Netflix to give back as much as several hundred dollars to its longest-subscribing Italia
stories.summary: A Rome court has ruled that Netflix's subscription price increases between 2017 and 2024 were unlawful, violating Italy's consumer code. The court ordered Netflix to refund its Italian subscribers up to €500 (approximately $576), depending on their plan. The refunds apply to price hikes dating back to 2017 and affect millions of Italian subscribers. The court also ordered Netflix to publish the ruling on its Italian website and in leading national newspapers. Netflix plans to appeal the decision. According to Italian law, any price increases issued or to be issued by Netflix after April 2025 are considered legal. The ruling only affects price hikes prior to this date, with the court ordering refunds for affected customers. Netflix will likely contest the decision, but for now, Italian subscribers may be eligible for refunds.

stories.headline: Sydney councils fear new datacentres could cause blackouts, block housing and affect locals’ health Datacentres ‘directly competing’ with possible residential builds near public transport, one council tells NSW inquiry, amid growing concerns
stories.summary: Sydney councils are expressing concerns about the impact of new datacentres on the community. They fear that these facilities could cause blackouts, block housing developments, and negatively affect locals' health. A council has told a NSW inquiry that datacentres are "directly competing" with possible residential builds near public transport, highlighting the potential for datacentres to limit housing options. The concerns come amid growing worries about the effects of datacentres on the environment and community. Datacentre project cancellations reportedly quadrupled in 2025, suggesting that communities are pushing back against these developments. The NSW inquiry is examining the issues surrounding datacentres, including their impact on housing and health. Councils are seeking to balance the needs of the community with the demands of the tech industry. The inquiry's findings will inform future decisions on datacentre developments in the region.

stories.headline: Annie Altman Files Amended Lawsuit Against Brother Sam Altman In Federal Court As Filing Revives Explosive Abuse Allegations
stories.summary: Annie Altman has filed an amended lawsuit in St. Louis federal court accusing her brother, OpenAI CEO Sam Altman, of sexual abuse beginning when she was 3 years old. Sam Altman denies the allegations and is countersuing for defamation. The original lawsuit was dismissed due to being filed too late, but the judge allowed Annie Altman to refile under Missouri law. Sam Altman claims his family has been supporting Annie financially, but her lawsuit amounts to extortion. He co-founded OpenAI and gained prominence after the release of the AI chatbot ChatGPT in 2022. The amended lawsuit revives the explosive abuse allegations, with Sam Altman maintaining his denial and pursuing a defamation counterclaim against his sister. The case is now moving forward in federal court.

stories.headline: OpenAI makes surprise media acquisition ahead of IPO. Why it will fail.
stories.summary: OpenAI, the developer of ChatGPT, has made a surprise acquisition of online talk show TBPN. The deal has raised eyebrows in the tech industry as it does not directly align with OpenAI's core focus on artificial intelligence. This move comes ahead of OpenAI's initial public offering (IPO). The acquisition has sparked concerns about editorial independence and the potential risks of media and entertainment transactions, which are often prone to failure. OpenAI had recently shut down its unprofitable Sora video-generation tools, citing high AI chip consumption. The company has not disclosed the motivations behind the TBPN acquisition, leaving many to speculate about its implications for OpenAI's future and its ability to integrate the talk show into its AI-centric business strategy. The deal's potential impact on OpenAI's IPO remains to be seen.

NPR is offering news, audio, and podcasts on breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, and science. The New York Times is covering major events with live news, investigations, and opinion pieces from over 150 countries. The New York Post is reporting on the latest breaking news from across the US. Al Jazeera is providing fact-based news, exclusive video footage, and updated maps to keep readers informed. Only two headlines are available for this breaking news story, with further updates expected to follow. stories.summary: News coverage is available from various sources, including NPR, The New York Times, The Washington Post, and Reuters. These outlets provide breaking news, live coverage, and in-depth investigations on national and international events, politics, business, science, and technology. The New York Times offers coverage from over 150 countries, while Reuters provides international news coverage from around the globe.
